source_label: Maximum privilege request spanning cloud infrastructure, email, files, and user administration
Kitchen-sink scope request spanning six unrelated services.
A desktop email client requires full mailbox access to display, compose, and delete emails
Webmail managers need to read and modify emails (move, archive, label) and manage labels for folder-like organization.
A helpdesk reads incoming support emails, drafts responses using templates, and sends replies.
A CRM reads emails to log conversation history, sends outreach emails, reads contacts to build the lead database, and accesses calendar events to track meetings.
Sales platforms need write access to contacts, email sending, calendar access, and app-specific Drive access.
A read-only CRM sync only ingests data without modifying the user's account
A project management tool syncs calendar events to show deadlines on a project timeline, syncs tasks for to-do tracking, and attaches files from Drive to cards or tickets.
Some open-source tools store their board state in a Google Sheet rather than a dedicated database
Time trackers only need to observe the user's calendar and tasks to log time automatically.
A backup tool must access the user's complete data across all services.
Migration tools need full Drive access plus explicit Docs and Sheets scopes to preserve native document formatting during cross-platform transfers.
An email archiver reads all mail and optionally reads Drive attachments for a complete archive.
A mail merge reads recipient lists from a spreadsheet, sends personalized emails, and accesses Drive for email template files.
Similar to mail merge but uses contacts for recipient data instead of or in addition to a spreadsheet.
General-purpose automation platforms need broad access because they execute user-defined workflows that may span multiple services
A form auto-responder watches for new form submissions, logs responses to a spreadsheet for record-keeping, and sends a confirmation email to each respondent.
These tools pull Google Analytics data and write it to a spreadsheet for custom dashboarding.
For large-scale analytics, data is piped from Google Analytics into BigQuery for SQL-based analysis.
Security teams export Workspace audit logs to spreadsheets for compliance reporting.
These tools read video files from Drive and upload them to YouTube on a schedule.
Creator dashboards need full YouTube access for channel management plus analytics for performance metrics.
Photo organizers sync between Google Photos and Drive folders, requiring full access to both services.
A learning management system integration needs to read and create courses, access class rosters for enrollment sync, attach files to assignments via Drive, and create quizzes via Forms.
A grade notification tool reads student coursework and grades, exports them to a spreadsheet for record-keeping, and sends email notifications to students or parents.
A reminder bot reads upcoming calendar events and sends notification messages into Chat spaces.
A file sharing bot allows users to search and share Drive files directly within Chat.
The minimal sign-in scope set used by virtually every web application offering Google SSO.
Some OAuth libraries request the explicit userinfo scopes alongside the shorthand scopes.
IT admin tools manage user accounts, group memberships, and security settings.
Security dashboards need read-only access to the user directory (to identify accounts) and audit logs.
Domain migration requires user management (to recreate accounts), data transfer, and domain provisioning.
Data pipelines move data between Cloud Storage and BigQuery.
